sheet.title: DOL Starter — Workshop Grinder M1 (11 kW)

items.rating: Auxiliary NO 13-14 hold contact (mechanically linked to K1)
Class 10, 16-24 A range, set 21 A (motor FLA)
START — green flush actuator, NO contact 13-14
STOP — red mushroom actuator, NC contact 21-22 wired in coil-break path
Incoming power terminal block L1/L2/L3, 16 mm² DIN-rail
Motor-tail outgoing terminal block U/V/W, 6 mm² DIN-rail
Control-supply terminal block L/N 230 VAC, 2.5 mm² DIN-rail

connections.function: Incoming L1 to isolator pole 1
Incoming L2 to isolator pole 2
Incoming L3 to isolator pole 3
Isolated L1 to contactor main pole 1
Isolated L2 to contactor main pole 2
Isolated L3 to contactor main pole 3
Contactor pole 1 to overload thermal element phase L1
Contactor pole 2 to overload thermal element phase L2
Contactor pole 3 to overload thermal element phase L3
Overload outgoing L1 to motor terminal U via outgoing tail
Overload outgoing L2 to motor terminal V via outgoing tail
Overload outgoing L3 to motor terminal W via outgoing tail
Motor-tail U to motor M1 terminal U
Motor-tail V to motor M1 terminal V
Motor-tail W to motor M1 terminal W
Control-supply L to overload trip-contact 95 (NC, opens on trip)
Overload 96 to STOP push-button NC contact terminal 21
STOP 22 to START push-button NO terminal 13
START 14 to contactor K1 coil A1 (energise path)
K1 coil A2 to control neutral N
Hold-in path: STOP 22 to K1-AUX terminal 13 (parallels START)
K1-AUX 14 holds coil A1 energised after START release
K1-AUX 14 also drives green RUN lamp H1 X1
Lamp H1 X2 returns to control neutral N

labels.text: K1 hold parallels S1 — release-safe once K1 picks

sequence_of_operation: START sequence: Operator presses S1 (momentary NO 13-14 closes) → control-supply L flows X3 → F1 95-96 (NC, closed because no trip) → S2 21-22 (NC, closed because not pressed) → S1 13-14 (closed) → K1 coil A1-A2 energises → K1 main poles 1/2 + 3/4 + 5/6 close → motor M1 receives 415 V 3-phase via F1 thermal element → motor accelerates from 147 A inrush down to 21 A FLA in ~4 s. K1-AUX 13-14 (mechanically linked NO) closes simultaneously and provides hold-in current bypassing S1, so K1 stays energised after S1 release. K1-AUX also energises H1 green RUN lamp via X3-N. STOP sequence: Operator presses S2 → S2 21-22 opens (NC break) → coil A1-A2 path interrupted → K1 drops out → main poles open → motor coasts to stop → K1-AUX 13-14 opens → H1 extinguishes. TRIP sequence: Motor thermal overload (sustained current > 24 A) heats F1 bimetal → F1 95-96 opens (NC break) → coil path interrupted → identical drop-out as STOP. Restart requires manual F1 reset before S1 will re-energise.

assumptions: KS 1700:2018 Annex E §VIII adopts BS EN 60617 verbatim for graphical symbols and BS EN 60947-4-1 for contactor utilisation categories AC-3 (squirrel-cage motor switching).

Type D MCB upstream (32 A) covers motor inrush 147 A within instantaneous trip band 10-20×In = 320-640 A per BS EN 60898-1:2019; 147 A inrush sits below the 10×In magnetic threshold so the upstream MCB will NOT nuisance-trip on start.
Overload Class 10 appropriate for 4 s start time: Class 10 tolerates locked-rotor current for ~10 s at 7.2×Ie — motor pulls 147 A (7×FLA) for 4 s, well inside the Class 10 thermal envelope.
Workshop ambient assumed ≤40 °C nominal; overload setting 21 A matches motor FLA at the IEC reference ambient — no ambient derating applied because the workshop is mechanically ventilated.

Leaf-mode execution; no db-layout-rollup, fault-level, or earthing intents available in this project — upstream OCPD context, prospective fault current, and PE bonding all engineer-declared via inputs.json.

rationale.chat_summary: DOL motor starter for 11 kW workshop grinder M1, KE jurisdiction (Nairobi industrial). K1 32A AC-3 contactor + F1 Class 10 thermal overload set 21 A (motor FLA) + Q1 32A 3P manual isolator; upstream 32A Type D MCB declared by engineer covers the 147 A inrush. Leaf-mode execution — no db-layout-rollup / fault-level / earthing intents in this project. Reply to refine (e.g. add anti-condensation heater or local emergency stop).

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: control_motor_starter (DOL topology) on sheet SHT-001 for a Nairobi industrial light-engineering workshop. Subject is M1, an 11 kW / 415 V / 21 A FLA 3-phase squirrel-cage induction motor driving a tool-room grinder. Leaf-mode execution: no upstream skills produced intents this schematic could consume, so all upstream context (32 A Type D MCB, supply class, earthing system) is engineer-declared via inputs.json and recorded in compliance_summary.assumptions[]. KE jurisdiction applies KS 1700:2018 with Annex E §VIII routing to BS EN 60617 (symbols) and BS EN 60947-4-1 (contactor categories).

decisions.label: DOL topology for 11 kW grinder
decisions.summary: 11 kW sits at the practical upper edge of DOL — inrush (~7×FLA = 147 A) is acceptable on an industrial 32 A Type D circuit, and DOL is the lowest-cost / lowest-component-count starter for a workshop tool that does not require torque ramping.
decisions.rule: Starter-topology selection by motor rating and load profile

decisions.label: Leaf-mode execution declared
decisions.summary: No db-layout-rollup / fault-level / earthing intents present; upstream context engineer-declared.
decisions.rule: Schematic skill leaf-mode fallback per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: Engineer-declared upstream: 32 A Type D MCB on a workshop sub-DB (DB-WS), 415 V 3-phase TN-S supply, 21 A motor FLA, 147 A locked-rotor inrush. Type D curve selected upstream because the 147 A inrush ÷ 32 A rating = 4.6×In, which would routinely nuisance-trip a Type B (3-5×In magnetic) or a Type C at the lower end of its 5-10×In band but sits well inside the Type D 10-20×In instantaneous band. No db-layout-rollup intent was available, so the upstream rating is captured as an inputs.json assumption rather than a consumed value.

decisions.label: Type D upstream MCB matches motor inrush profile
decisions.summary: 147 A inrush / 32 A In = 4.6×In — below Type D's 10×In magnetic threshold so no nuisance trip; above Type B/C bands which would trip.
decisions.rule: MCB curve selection for motor circuits

decisions.label: 415 V 3-phase TN-S engineer-declared
decisions.summary: No earthing intent consumed — TN-S is the dominant Kenyan utility arrangement for industrial premises with a dedicated PE conductor from the substation.

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 11 devices placed: Q1 (32A 3-pole manual lockable isolator for safe maintenance isolation), K1 (32A AC-3 contactor — AC-3 is the canonical utilisation category for squirrel-cage motor switching), K1-AUX (mechanically linked NO 13/14 auxiliary for hold-in), F1 (Class 10 thermal overload set at 21 A motor FLA), M1 (the 11 kW 3-phase induction motor), S1 (green NO START), S2 (red mushroom STOP wired through NC contact 21-22), H1 (green RUN indicator lamp), plus three terminal rails X1/X2/X3 segregating incoming power, motor-tail outgoing power, and 230 VAC control supply. The S2 push-button physically uses the NO actuator from the symbol library but is wired as a coil-break (NC) — labelled explicitly as 21-22 contact terminals in the label set.

decisions.label: K1 32A AC-3 contactor for 11 kW motor
decisions.summary: AC-3 utilisation category covers squirrel-cage motor starting + running + occasional inching at full load.

decisions.label: F1 Class 10 thermal overload set 21 A
decisions.summary: Class 10 tolerates locked-rotor current ~10 s at 7.2×Ie — workshop grinder pulls 147 A (7×FLA) for ~4 s start, well inside Class 10 envelope. Setpoint 21 A matches motor FLA at IEC reference ambient.
decisions.code_clause: BS EN 60947-4-1 § 4.7 (overload relay classes) + IEC 60947-4-1 Table 2

decisions.label: Q1 manual lockable isolator for maintenance
decisions.summary: Workshop maintenance regime requires safe-isolation point local to the motor — lockable handle satisfies the safe-isolation requirement without depending on remote DB isolation.

sections.title: Connection Topology
sections.summary: Three logical circuits across 24 connections. Power (W1-W15, power_LV, 6 mm² Cu): X1 → Q1 → K1 main poles → F1 thermal → X2 → M1, full 3-phase 415 V path. Control (W16-W22, control_LV, 1.5 mm² Cu, 230 VAC from one phase + N at X3): L → F1 95/96 (NC trip, opens on overload) → S2 21/22 (NC, opens on STOP) → S1 13/14 (NO, closes on START) → K1 A1 → A2 → N. Hold-in: K1-AUX 13/14 (NO, mechanically linked) parallels S1, so the coil stays energised after S1 release. Auxiliary (W23-W24, aux_AC): K1-AUX 14 drives H1 RUN lamp X1, H1 X2 returns to N. Canonical 3-wire control — any series break (F1 trip OR S2 press OR control loss) drops K1 out, fail-safe.

decisions.label: 3-wire hold-in via K1-AUX parallel to S1
decisions.summary: Canonical fail-safe control wiring — momentary START, latched until any series break.

decisions.label: Series break path F1 → S2 → S1/K1-AUX → K1 coil
decisions.summary: Overload OR Stop OR control-supply loss all drop K1 out — single failure mode in any series element is fail-safe.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against KS 1700:2018 § 552 (motor switching) routed via Annex E §VIII to BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 552, BS EN 60947-4-1 (LV switchgear — contactors & motor-starters), BS EN 60898-1:2019 (MCBs upstream), BS EN 60204-1 (machinery safety — control circuits), and IEC 60364-5-55 (electrical equipment — operating). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Five assumptions documented in compliance_summary.assumptions[]: Annex E §VIII routing, Type D inrush coverage, Class 10 thermal envelope, 40 °C ambient, and the explicit leaf-mode declaration.

decisions.label: KS 1700:2018 + Annex E §VIII routing form
decisions.summary: Every protection-related clause cites KS 1700:2018 with explicit `(Annex E §VIII: adopts BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § X.Y.Z verbatim)` routing — per Sprint 3-W2b citation discipline.

decisions.label: BS EN 60947-4-1 for contactor + overload
decisions.summary: Jurisdiction-agnostic standard for LV switchgear and controlgear; cited directly without routing.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Leaf-mode execution explicit — no db-layout-rollup / fault-level / earthing intents available, so upstream OCPD (32 A Type D), prospective fault current, and earthing system (TN-S) are engineer-declared via inputs.json and re-stated in compliance_summary.assumptions[]. Workshop-environment: ambient ≤40 °C with mechanical ventilation, no per-circuit derating; 6 mm² Cu power conductors sized against the 32 A upstream at IEC reference ambient (formal cable sizing belongs to cable-sizing skill). Motor-inrush coordination verified: 147 A is 4.6×In on the upstream 32 A Type D, well below the 10×In magnetic threshold per BS EN 60898-1:2019 Type D characteristic. Anti-condensation heater not specified — workshop heated, motor in dry environment.

decisions.label: Leaf-mode execution explicit
decisions.summary: No upstream intents — all upstream context engineer-declared via inputs.json.
decisions.rule: Schematic leaf-mode disclosure per generator prompt Step 11

decisions.label: Motor-inrush coordination 4.6×In on 32A Type D
decisions.summary: 147 A inrush ÷ 32 A In = 4.6×In — well below Type D 10×In threshold, no nuisance trip.
